## Installation

You can install autoReg package on github.

``` r

#install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("cardiomoon/autoReg")
```

## Load package

To load the package, use library() function.

``` r

library(autoReg)
```

## Main features

### 1.Summarizing baseline characteristics : gaze()

You can make a table summarizing baseline characteristics easily.

``` r
library(moonBook) # For use of example data acs
gaze(sex~.,data=acs)
————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
  Dependent:sex        levels           Female          Male        p   
       (N)                             (N=287)        (N=570)           
————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
age               Mean ± SD             68.7 ± 10.7   60.6 ± 11.2  <.001 
cardiogenicShock  No                    275 (95.8%)     530 (93%)   .136 
                  Yes                     12 (4.2%)       40 (7%)        
entry             Femoral               119 (41.5%)   193 (33.9%)   .035 
                  Radial                168 (58.5%)   377 (66.1%)        
Dx                NSTEMI                 50 (17.4%)   103 (18.1%)   .012 
                  STEMI                  84 (29.3%)   220 (38.6%)        
                  Unstable Angina       153 (53.3%)   247 (43.3%)        
EF                Mean ± SD             56.3 ± 10.1    55.6 ± 9.4   .387 
height            Mean ± SD             153.8 ± 6.2   167.9 ± 6.1  <.001 
weight            Mean ± SD              57.2 ± 9.3   68.7 ± 10.3  <.001 
BMI               Mean ± SD              24.2 ± 3.6    24.3 ± 3.2   .611 
obesity           No                    194 (67.6%)   373 (65.4%)   .580 
                  Yes                    93 (32.4%)   197 (34.6%)        
TC                Mean ± SD            188.9 ± 51.1  183.3 ± 45.9   .124 
LDLC              Mean ± SD            117.8 ± 41.2  116.0 ± 41.1   .561 
HDLC              Mean ± SD             39.0 ± 11.5   37.8 ± 10.9   .145 
TG                Mean ± SD            119.9 ± 76.2  127.9 ± 97.3   .195 
DM                No                    173 (60.3%)   380 (66.7%)   .077 
                  Yes                   114 (39.7%)   190 (33.3%)        
HBP               No                     83 (28.9%)   273 (47.9%)  <.001 
                  Yes                   204 (71.1%)   297 (52.1%)        
smoking           Ex-smoker              49 (17.1%)   155 (27.2%)  <.001 
                  Never                 209 (72.8%)   123 (21.6%)        
                  Smoker                 29 (10.1%)   292 (51.2%)        
————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
```

### For easy reproducible research : myft()

You can make a publication-ready table easily using myft(). It makes a
flextable object which can use in either HTML and PDF format.

``` r
library(dplyr) # for use of `%>%`

Attaching package: 'dplyr'
The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    filter, lag
The following objects are masked from 'package:base':

    intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
ft=gaze(sex~.,data=acs) %>% myft()
ft
```

![](reference/figures/unnamed-chunk-5-1.png)

You can also make a powerpoint file using rrtable::table2pptx()
function.

``` r

library(rrtable)

table2pptx(ft)
```

    Exported table as Report.pptx

You can make a microsoft word file using rrtable::table2docx() function.

``` r

table2docx(ft)
```

    Exported table as Report.docx

### Summarizing baseline characteristics with two or more grouping variables

You can get a table summarizing baseline characteristics with two or
more grouping variables.

``` r

gaze(sex+Dx~.,data=acs) %>% myft()
```

![](reference/figures/unnamed-chunk-8-1.png)

You can also use three or more grouping variables.The resultant table
will be too long to review, but you can try.

``` r

gaze(sex+DM+HBP~age,data=acs) %>% myft()
```

![](reference/figures/unnamed-chunk-9-1.png)

### 2. For automatic selection of explanatory variables : autoReg()

You can make a table summarizing results of regression analysis. For
example, let us perform a logistic regression with the colon cancer
data.

``` r
library(survival)   # For use of data colon
data(cancer)  

fit=glm(status~rx+sex+age+obstruct+perfor+nodes,data=colon,family="binomial")
summary(fit)

Call:
glm(formula = status ~ rx + sex + age + obstruct + perfor + nodes, 
    family = "binomial", data = colon)

Deviance Residuals: 
    Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  
-2.4950  -1.0594  -0.7885   1.1619   1.6424  

Coefficients:
             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
(Intercept) -0.645417   0.285558  -2.260   0.0238 *  
rxLev       -0.067422   0.118907  -0.567   0.5707    
rxLev+5FU   -0.627480   0.121684  -5.157 2.51e-07 ***
sex         -0.053541   0.098975  -0.541   0.5885    
age          0.002307   0.004234   0.545   0.5859    
obstruct     0.283703   0.125194   2.266   0.0234 *  
perfor       0.319281   0.292034   1.093   0.2743    
nodes        0.190563   0.018255  10.439  < 2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

(Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)

    Null deviance: 2525.4  on 1821  degrees of freedom
Residual deviance: 2342.4  on 1814  degrees of freedom
  (36 observations deleted due to missingness)
AIC: 2358.4

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 4
```

You can make table with above result.

``` r
autoReg(fit)
——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
 Dependent: status              0 (N=925)    1 (N=897)      OR (multivariable)     
——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
rx                 Obs          282 (30.5%)  342 (38.1%)                           
                   Lev          285 (30.8%)    323 (36%)  0.93 (0.74-1.18, p=.571) 
                   Lev+5FU      358 (38.7%)  232 (25.9%)  0.53 (0.42-0.68, p<.001) 
sex                Mean ± SD      0.5 ± 0.5    0.5 ± 0.5  0.95 (0.78-1.15, p=.589) 
age                Mean ± SD    60.1 ± 11.5  59.5 ± 12.3  1.00 (0.99-1.01, p=.586) 
obstruct           Mean ± SD      0.2 ± 0.4    0.2 ± 0.4  1.33 (1.04-1.70, p=.023) 
perfor             Mean ± SD      0.0 ± 0.2    0.0 ± 0.2  1.38 (0.78-2.47, p=.274) 
nodes              Mean ± SD      2.7 ± 2.4    4.6 ± 4.2  1.21 (1.17-1.26, p<.001) 
——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————
```

Or you can make a publication-ready table.

``` r

autoReg(fit) %>% myft()
```

![](reference/figures/unnamed-chunk-12-1.png)

If you want make a table with more explanation, you can make categorical
variables with numeric variables. For example, the explanatory variables
obstruct(obstruction of colon by tumor) and perfor(perforation of colon)
is coded as 0 or 1, but it is “No” or “Yes” actually. Also the dependent
variable status is coded as 0 or 1, it is “Alive” or “Died”.

``` r

colon$status.factor=factor(colon$status,labels=c("Alive","Died"))
colon$obstruct.factor=factor(colon$obstruct,labels=c("No","Yes"))
colon$perfor.factor=factor(colon$perfor,labels=c("No","Yes"))
colon$sex.factor=factor(colon$sex,labels=c("Female","Male"))

fit=glm(status.factor~rx+sex.factor+age+obstruct.factor+perfor.factor+nodes,data=colon,family="binomial")
result=autoReg(fit) 
result %>% myft()
```

![](reference/figures/unnamed-chunk-13-1.png)

You can add labels to the names of variables with setLabel() function.

``` r

colon$status.factor=setLabel(colon$status.factor,"Mortality")
colon$rx=setLabel(colon$rx,"Treatment")
colon$age=setLabel(colon$age,"Age(Years)")
colon$sex.factor=setLabel(colon$sex.factor,"Sex")
colon$obstruct.factor=setLabel(colon$obstruct.factor,"Obstruction")
colon$perfor.factor=setLabel(colon$perfor.factor,"Perforation")
colon$nodes=setLabel(colon$nodes,"Positive nodes")

fit=glm(status.factor~rx+sex.factor+age+obstruct.factor+perfor.factor+nodes,data=colon,family="binomial")
result=autoReg(fit) 
result %>% myft()
```

![](reference/figures/unnamed-chunk-14-1.png)

If you do not want to show the reference values in table, you can
shorten the table.

``` r

shorten(result) %>% myft()
```

![](reference/figures/unnamed-chunk-15-1.png)

### Add univariate models to table and automatic selection of explanatory variables

You can add the results of univariate analyses to the table. At this
time, the autoReg() function automatically select explanatory variables
below the threshold(default value 0.2) and perform multivariate
analysis. In this table, the p values of explanatory variables
sex.factor and age is above the default threshold(0.2), they are
excluded in multivariate model.

``` r

autoReg(fit, uni=TRUE) %>% myft()
```

![](reference/figures/unnamed-chunk-16-1.png)

If you want to include all explanatory variables in the multivariate
model, just set the threshold 1.

``` r

autoReg(fit, uni=TRUE,threshold=1) %>% myft()
```

![](reference/figures/unnamed-chunk-17-1.png)

You can perform stepwise backward elimination to select variables and
make a final model. Just set final=TRUE.

``` r

autoReg(fit, uni=TRUE,threshold=1, final=TRUE) %>% myft()
```

![](reference/figures/unnamed-chunk-18-1.png)

#### Multiple imputation with mice()

When the argument imputed=TRUE, autoReg() function make a multiple
imputed model using mice::mice() function. By default, 20 imputations
performed. If you want, you can change the number of imputations with m
argument.

``` r

autoReg(fit, imputed=TRUE) %>% myft()
```

![](reference/figures/unnamed-chunk-19-1.png)

### Summarize regression model results in a plot : modelPlot()

You can draw the plot summarizing the model with modelPlot()

``` r

x=modelPlot(fit)
x
```

![](reference/figures/unnamed-chunk-20-1.png)

You can make powerpoint file with this plot using rrtable::plot2pptx().

``` r

plot2pptx(print(x))
```

    Exported plot as Report.pptx

You can summarize models in a plot. If you want to summarize univariate
and multivariate model in a plot, just set the uni=TRUE and adjust the
threshold. You can decide whether or not show the reference by show.ref
argument.

``` r

modelPlot(fit,uni=TRUE,threshold=1,show.ref=FALSE)
```

![](reference/figures/unnamed-chunk-22-1.png)

# Package index

## All functions

- [`OEplot()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/OEplot.md)
  : Draw an Observed vs Expected plot
- [`addFitSummary()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/addFitSummary.md)
  : Add model summary to an object of class gaze
- [`addLabelData()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/addLabelData.md)
  : Add labels to data
- [`adjustedPlot()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/adjustedPlot.md)
  : Draw an expected plot
- [`adjustedPlot.survreg()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/adjustedPlot.survreg.md)
  : Draw predicted survival curve with an object survreg
- [`adjustedPlot2()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/adjustedPlot2.md)
  : Draw a survfitted plot
- [`adjustedPlot2.survreg()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/adjustedPlot2.survreg.md)
  : Draw predicted survival curve as a ggplot with an object survreg
- [`anderson`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/anderson.md)
  : Remission survival times of 42 leukemia patients
- [`anderson1`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/anderson1.md)
  : Remission survival times of 42 leukemia patients
- [`anderson2`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/anderson2.md)
  : Remission survival times of 31 leukemia patients
- [`as_printable()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/as_printable.md)
  : Convert data.frame to printable form
- [`autoReg()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/autoReg.md)
  : Perform univariable and multivariable regression and stepwise
  backward regression automatically
- [`autoRegCox()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/autoRegCox.md)
  : perform automatic regression for a class of coxph
- [`autoReg_sub()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/autoReg_sub.md)
  : Perform univariable and multivariable regression and stepwise
  backward regression automatically
- [`autoRegsurvreg()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/autoRegsurvreg.md)
  : perform automatic regression for a class of survreg
- [`beNumeric()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/beNumeric.md)
  : Whether a string vector can be converted to numeric
- [`bootPredict()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/bootPredict.md)
  : Bootstrap simulation for model prediction
- [`countGroups()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/countGroups.md)
  : Count groups
- [`coxzphplot()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/coxzphplot.md)
  : Graphical Test of Proportional Hazards
- [`crr2stats()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/crr2stats.md)
  : Extract statistics from an object of class crr
- [`crrFormula()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/crrFormula.md)
  : Competing Risk Regression with Formula
- [`descNum()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/descNum.md)
  : Make description for numeric summary
- [`df2flextable()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/df2flextable.md)
  : Convert data.frame to flextable
- [`drawline()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/drawline.md)
  : draw line character
- [`expectedPlot()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/expectedPlot.md)
  : Draw an adjusted Plot for a numeric predictor
- [`filldown()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/filldown.md)
  : filldown vector with lead value
- [`find1stDup()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/find1stDup.md)
  : Find first duplicated position
- [`findDup()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/findDup.md)
  : Find duplicated term
- [`fit2final()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/fit2final.md)
  : Make final model using stepwise backward elimination
- [`fit2lik()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/fit2lik.md)
  : extract likelihood information with a coxph object
- [`fit2list()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/fit2list.md)
  : Make a list of univariable model with multivariable regression model
- [`fit2model()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/fit2model.md)
  : Restore fit model data containing AsIs expressions
- [`fit2multi()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/fit2multi.md)
  : Make multivariable regression model by selecting univariable models
  with p.value below threshold
- [`fit2newdata()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/fit2newdata.md)
  : Make a new data of mean value or most frequent value
- [`fit2stats()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/fit2stats.md)
  : Summarize statistics with a model
- [`fit2summary()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/fit2summary.md)
  : Summarize statistics with a model or model list
- [`gaze()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/gaze.md) :
  Produce table for descriptive statistics
- [`gaze(`*`<formula_sub>`*`)`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/gaze.formula_sub.md)
  : Produce table for descriptive statistics
- [`gazeCat()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/gazeCat.md)
  : Summary function for categorical variable
- [`gazeCont()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/gazeCont.md)
  : Summary function for continuous variable
- [`gaze_sub()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/gaze_sub.md)
  : Summary function for categorical/continuous variable
- [`getInteraction()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/getInteraction.md)
  : Get interaction data from data
- [`getN()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/getN.md) :
  Get number of data specified by 'name' and 'desc'
- [`getSigVars()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/getSigVars.md)
  : Get explanatory variables of a model with significance level below
  the threshold
- [`ggcmprsk()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/ggcmprsk.md)
  : Draw Cumulative Incidence Curves for Competing Risks
- [`ggcmprsk2()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/ggcmprsk2.md)
  : Compare cumulative incidence to th Kaplan-Meier estimate
- [`highlight2()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/highlight2.md)
  : Highlight a data.frame
- [`imputedReg()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/imputedReg.md)
  : Make a multiple imputed model
- [`is.mynumeric()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/is.mynumeric.md)
  : Decide whether a vector can be treated as a numeric variable
- [`label_parse()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/label_parse.md)
  : takes the breaks as input and returns labels as output
- [`loglogplot()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/loglogplot.md)
  : Draw log-log plot
- [`maxnchar()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/maxnchar.md)
  : Return maximum character number except NA
- [`modelPlot()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/modelPlot.md)
  : Draw coefficients/odds ratio/hazard ratio plot
- [`modelsSummary()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/modelsSummary.md)
  : Makes table summarizing list of models
- [`modelsSummaryTable()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/modelsSummaryTable.md)
  : Makes flextable summarizing list of models
- [`my.chisq.test2()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/my.chisq.test2.md)
  : Statistical test for categorical variables Statistical test for
  categorical variables
- [`my.t.test2()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/my.t.test2.md)
  : Statistical test for continuous variables
- [`mycphSimple()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/mycphSimple.md)
  : Fit Simple Proportional Hazards Regression Model
- [`myformat()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/myformat.md)
  : Convert data.frame to printable format
- [`myft()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/myft.md) :
  Convert data.frame into flextable
- [`mysurvregSimple()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/mysurvregSimple.md)
  : Fit Simple AFT Model
- [`num2factor()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/num2factor.md)
  : Convert a numeric column in a data.frame to a factor
- [`num2stat()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/num2stat.md)
  : Summarize numeric vector to statistical summary
- [`p2character2()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/p2character2.md)
  : Change p value to string
- [`print(`*`<autoReg>`*`)`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/print.autoReg.md)
  : S3 method print for an object of class autoReg
- [`print(`*`<gaze>`*`)`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/print.gaze.md)
  : S3 method print for an object of class gaze
- [`print(`*`<modelPlot>`*`)`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/print.modelPlot.md)
  : S3 method for an class modelPlot
- [`printdf()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/printdf.md)
  : Print function for data.frame
- [`removeDup()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/removeDup.md)
  : Remove duplicated term
- [`residualNull()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/residualNull.md)
  : Make a residual plot of NULL model
- [`residualPlot()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/residualPlot.md)
  : Draw a residual plot with an object of class coxph
- [`restoreData()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/restoreData.md)
  : restore data with factor in column name
- [`restoreData2()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/restoreData2.md)
  : restore data with I() in column name
- [`restoreData3()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/restoreData3.md)
  : restore data with operator in column name
- [`revOperator()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/revOperator.md)
  : get opposite arithmetic operator
- [`roundDf()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/roundDf.md)
  : Convert numeric columns of data.frame to character
- [`setLabel()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/setLabel.md)
  : Add label to a vector
- [`shorten()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/shorten.md)
  : Shorten an object of class gaze
- [`showEffect()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/showEffect.md)
  : Show effects of covariates
- [`strata2df()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/strata2df.md)
  : Convert a character vector to a data.frame
- [`survfit2df()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/survfit2df.md)
  : Extract survival data from an object of class "survfit"
- [`survreg2final()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/survreg2final.md)
  : Make final model using stepwise backward elimination
- [`survreg2multi()`](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/reference/survreg2multi.md)
  : Make multivariable regression model by selecting univariable models
  with p.value below threshold

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [Automatic Regression
  Modeling](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/articles/Automatic_Regression_Modeling.md):
- [Bootstrap Simulation for model
  prediction](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/articles/Bootstrap_Prediction.md):
- [Getting
  started](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/articles/Getting_started.md):
- [Statistical tests in
  gaze](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/articles/Statiastical_test_in_gaze.md):
- [Survival
  Analysis](https://cardiomoon.github.io/autoReg/articles/Survival.md):
